title: dianthus orientalis subsp. orientalis
intro: Dianthus orientalis subsp. orientalis, commonly known as the Oriented Pink or the Chinese Pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to China and Eastern Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Dianthus orientalis subsp. orientalis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
